behaviors.description: Out-of-stock products are excluded from final recommendations even if their relevance score is high.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is filter->ranker pipeline handoff (MOCK-MISS): an out-of-stock item can leak back in after ranking/merge. No behavior text requires real OS/network/subprocess (REAL-INFRA=no), no AI/ML output-quality dependence is stated (LLM-DEP=no), and no multi-agentic chain (MULTI-STEP=no). Rejected higher levels because behavior only states exclusion logic inside recommendation assembly, not external infrastructure/model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire catalog filter + ranking modules with an in-process catalog fixture containing high-score out-of-stock items; assert none appear in returned page.

behaviors.description: Blocked categories in user settings are excluded from recommendations.
behaviors.justification: Bug appears at settings->filter boundary (MOCK-MISS): category blocks can be dropped or misapplied when modules compose. No real OS/network/subprocess required (REAL-INFRA=no), no dependence on model output quality (LLM-DEP=no), no multi-step agentic flow (MULTI-STEP=no). Rejected System/Agentic because behavior text is deterministic policy enforcement from user settings.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process user-profile/settings module plus recommendation filter; seed blocked and allowed categories and assert blocked categories are absent.

behaviors.description: Products matching recent browsing patterns rank above products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on recommender scoring quality from behavioral signals (LLM-DEP=yes for real AI/ML scoring behavior): a stub scorer can always satisfy ordering and hide real model failure/drift. Not Workflow because this is one model-driven ranking decision, not multiple agentic stages. Rejected Integration because behavior text is about ranking outcome quality, not only routing/retry/payload plumbing.
behaviors.test_description: Run against the real recommendation model/scorer with fixed user history and candidate set; verify statistically that browsing-matched items outrank no-signal items across repeated evaluations/tolerance bands.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns correct page slices with stable ordering; repeated page-2 requests are identical when data is unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at rank->paginate->response composition boundary (MOCK-MISS). Can be reproduced with in-process modules and fixed dataset; no required real OS/network/subprocess (REAL-INFRA=no), no model-quality dependency (LLM-DEP=no), no multi-agentic chain (MULTI-STEP=no). Rejected System because behavior text explicitly constrains unchanged underlying data, allowing deterministic local verification.
behaviors.test_description: Build in-process fixture with deterministic ranked list and unchanged snapshot; request page 2 twice and assert identical IDs/order and correct offset/limit semantics.

behaviors.description: Recommendations include at least one item from an unseen category (serendipity requirement).
behaviors.justification: This is output-quality behavior of recommendation strategy (novelty/diversity), which depends on real model/scoring decisions (LLM-DEP=yes). Deterministic mocks can force-pass and hide true relevance-diversity tradeoff failures (MOCK-HIDE=yes). Not Workflow: single recommendation decision, not sequential agentic handoffs.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate real recommender on users with narrow browsing history; assert at least one top-N recommendation belongs to unseen categories while relevance guardrails remain acceptable.

behaviors.description: Cold-start users receive popularity-based recommendations instead of empty results.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is fallback orchestration between history detection and popularity source (MOCK-MISS). Can be validated with local modules/data; no mandatory OS/network/subprocess (REAL-INFRA=no). Behavior text specifies rule-based fallback, not model-quality judgment (LLM-DEP not required). Rejected Agentic because disqualifying text is explicit: 'based on overall popularity rather than an empty result set' (policy fallback).
behaviors.test_description: Create a no-history user fixture, wire recommender + popularity provider in-process, assert non-empty list and that ranking source is popularity path.

behaviors.description: Click tracking stores correct product ID, list position, and timestamp for each clicked recommendation.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears in handoff from recommendation response context to tracking write path (MOCK-MISS). In-process store and injected clock can catch mapping errors; no real OS/network/subprocess is required by behavior text (REAL-INFRA=no). No AI/ML output dependency (LLM-DEP=no), no multi-agentic sequence (MULTI-STEP=no). Rejected System since real infra is not necessary to detect incorrect fields.
behaviors.test_description: Trigger recommendation display + click event through application modules; assert persisted event has exact product_id, rank_position, and expected timestamp from test clock.

behaviors.description: Recommendation generation stays under 200ms for a 100,000-product catalog.
behaviors.justification: Performance SLA depends on real runtime/OS characteristics (REAL-INFRA=yes): CPU scheduling, memory pressure, query execution, and serialization costs are often hidden by in-process fakes (MOCK-HIDE=yes). Not Agentic/Workflow because requirement is latency bound, not model decision quality or multi-step agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end recommendation requests in a real process with 100k realistic catalog data on target-like environment; measure p95/p99 latency and assert under 200ms threshold.

behaviors.description: After a user purchases a recommended product, subsequent recommendations deprioritize that product.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is purchase-event ingestion -> user state update -> ranking pipeline (MOCK-MISS). This cross-module state handoff is testable with local data stores and deterministic scoring. No explicit need for real OS/network/subprocess (REAL-INFRA=no). Behavior does not require judging model quality beyond policy effect, so Agentic not strictly required from text.
behaviors.test_description: Simulate recommendation response, then record purchase of one recommended item; request recommendations again and assert purchased item rank drops relative to prior ranking.

behaviors.description: With unchanged user state, repeated recommendation requests return identical ranked lists.
behaviors.justification: Determinism failures usually arise from module interactions (feature assembly, sorting tie-breakers, cache/state reads) rather than a single pure function (MOCK-MISS). Can be caught in-process with fixed seeds/snapshots. No mandatory real OS/network/subprocess (REAL-INFRA=no), no required AI/ML quality judgment from text, and no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Freeze user/context state and random seeds, invoke recommendation endpoint twice through integrated modules, and assert exact list identity and order.
